chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمِنْبَرِ وَ الرَّوْضَةِ وَ مَقَامِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله )
chapter_title.en: Chapter 217 – The Pulpit, and the garden, and place of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/267)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (8/267)
text: حَدُّ الرَّوْضَةِ فِي مَسْجِدِ الرَّسُولِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) إِلَى طَرَفِ الظِّلَالِ وَ حَدُّ الْمَسْجِدِ إِلَى الْأُسْطُوَانَتَيْنِ عَنْ يَمِينِ الْمِنْبَرِ إِلَى الطَّرِيقِ مِمَّا يَلِي سُوقَ اللَّيْلِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ismail, from Ali Bin Al Nu’man, from Abdullah Bin Muskan, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘A boundary of the Garden in the Masjid of Rasool<sup>saww</sup> is up to the side of the shaded area, and a boundary of the Masjid is up to the two pillars from the right of the Pulpit up to the street from what follows the night market’.
en.sarwar: 6. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Muhammad ibn Isma‘il from Ali ibn al-Nu’man from ‘Abd Allah ibn Muskan from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the limit of al-Rawdah in the Masjid of the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, is to the edge of the shadows. The limit of the Masjid is up to two pillars from the right side of the pulpit up to the pathway in front of the night Suq (market).”
